name: Zetes
description: Zetes, son of Boreas, the North Wind, and Oreithyia, was a winged hero in Greek mythology, known for his incredible speed and his role alongside his brother Calais in the Argonautic expedition. Born in Thrace, Zetes possessed the power of flight, a gift inherited from his father, which made him a formidable figure. He is most famously recognized for his pursuit and torment of the Harpies, monstrous bird-women who plagued King Phineus of Thrace, stealing his food and defiling his meals. Zetes and Calais, acting on the pleas of Phineus, chased the Harpies across the seas, eventually driving them away, although accounts differ on whether they were killed, captured, or merely swore to leave Phineus in peace. Their intervention was crucial in allowing Phineus to share his prophetic knowledge with the Argonauts. Zetes's participation in the Argonautic expedition highlights his bravery and commitment to aiding those in need. His winged nature and swiftness made him a valuable asset to the crew, contributing to their success in navigating treacherous waters and overcoming various challenges. Zetes's story exemplifies the heroic qualities of courage, loyalty, and the willingness to confront monstrous forces to protect the innocent, solidifying his place as a significant figure in Greek mythology.
subtitle: Winged hero of the Argonauts

keyMyths.title: The Harpies' Bane
keyMyths.description: Zetes and his brother Calais chased the Harpies away from King Phineus, saving him from starvation and allowing him to reveal vital prophecies. This act demonstrated their courage and speed.

keyMyths.title: The Argonautic Expedition
keyMyths.description: Zetes joined Jason and the Argonauts on their quest for the Golden Fleece, utilizing his winged speed to aid the crew in their travels. His abilities made him a valuable asset during the voyage.

keyMyths.title: Son of the North Wind
keyMyths.description: Zetes inherited his winged ability from his father, Boreas, the North Wind. This divine parentage granted him unique powers and a connection to the natural world.

sources.author: Apollonius Rhodius
sources.description: Primary source detailing Zetes's role in the Argonautic expedition and his defeat of the Harpies.
sources.text: Primary source detailing Zetes's role in the Argonautic expedition and his defeat of the Harpies.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: Provides information on Zetes's parentage and other mythological details.
